justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation via cell-based architecture with no raw data crossing regions. GDPR and CCPA data residency addressed explicitly with physical network isolation. Global dashboard consumes only sanitized/anonymized metrics via the Outbound Sanitizer and Global Event Bridge — no cross-region raw data access. Courier assignment regional isolation enforced via Assignment aggregate with hard region-match invariant. Trade-offs thoroughly cover 3-region operational overhead (configuration drift, progressive deployment, IaC duplication). Degraded EU service during outage explicitly designed with cached reads, no new orders, and defined staleness SLOs.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough cell-based architecture with clear PII boundaries enforced at infrastructure level rather than application level — eliminates entire class of compliance bugs
Outbound Sanitizer with deny-by-default allowlist is a strong design for preventing accidental PII leakage to the global plane
Account-region binding correctly identified as necessary over pure geo-routing for data residency compliance, with traveling customer latency acknowledged as acceptable trade-off
Customer relocation scenario (EU→US migration) addressed as a dedicated risk with a formal protocol — shows depth of thinking beyond the happy path
Operational overhead of 3 regions covered in detail: configuration drift risk, progressive rollout strategy, IaC parameterization, and GitOps deployment pipeline
Correctly noted that APAC regulations vary by country and may require sub-regional cells — demonstrates awareness beyond just GDPR/CCPA

CCPA addressed implicitly through the general regional isolation model rather than explicitly calling out California-specific requirements (e.g., consumer deletion rights, opt-out mechanisms) — adequate but could be more specific
Defense-in-depth for PII leakage risk: schema validation, secondary audit scanner, and CI compliance tests — production-grade thinking
